title: PlantEncyclopediaEntry
description: Wikipedia-style encyclopedia entry schema for a plant genus or species.

type.description: The taxonomic rank or entry type this record represents.

name.description: Common name of the genus or species.

scientific_name.description: Italicised Latin scientific name (genus or genus + species epithet).

authority.description: Taxonomic authority who first described the taxon (e.g., 'L.' for Linnaeus).

division.description: Also called Phylum in some classifications.

species.description: List of accepted species within this genus (leave empty if entry is for a species).

description.description: Opening encyclopedic summary paragraph (Wikipedia lead section style).

etymology.description: Origin and meaning of the scientific name.

notes.description: Additional conservation notes or per-species variation details.

native_range.description: List of countries or regions where the taxon is native.

habitat.description: Typical habitat description (e.g., tropical rainforest, savanna, rocky slopes).

growth_form.description: General growth form (e.g., tree, shrub, herb, climber).

height_m.description: Typical height range in metres (e.g., '1–5 m').

stem.description: Description of the stem or trunk.

leaves.description: Description of the leaves (shape, arrangement, texture, colour).

seeds_or_spores.description: Description of seeds, fruits, or spores.

roots.description: Root system description, including specialised structures (e.g., coralloid roots in cycads).

pollination.description: Pollination mechanism (e.g., wind, insect, specific pollinator).

symbioses.description: Notable symbiotic relationships (e.g., cyanobacterial nitrogen fixation in cycads).

food.description: Edible parts and food preparation methods.

horticultural.description: Use in gardens, landscaping, or as ornamental plants.

hardiness_zones.description: USDA or RHS hardiness zone range.

light.description: Light requirements (e.g., full sun, partial shade).

propagation.description: Common propagation methods (seeds, offsets, cuttings, tissue culture).

notes.description: Additional cultivation notes or common problems.

threats_and_conservation.description: Detailed description of threats (habitat loss, over-collection, climate change) and conservation measures.

notable_species.description: A curated list of the most notable or well-known species within the genus.

gallery.description: Placeholder list of images for the entry.

external_links.description: External web resources (e.g., IUCN, Plants of the World Online, GBIF).

categories.description: Encyclopedic category tags for this entry.

last_edited.description: Date this entry was last edited (YYYY-MM-DD).
